PT school taught you how to treat patients, but did it teach you how to start a physical therapy business?

Seven years later: four brick-and-mortar clinics, fully bootstrapped.

videoDetails.title: How to use ChatGPT to write emails that don't sound like robots
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Telling ChatGPT to "fix this" isn't a prompt. It's a guess.

Adelle from the Prehab Guys learned that the hard way, and the moment she figured out how to use ChatGPT to write emails that actually land the right tone, she was sold on AI. The trick: specificity. Instead of "make this less harsh," try "rewrite this on a scale of 1 to 10, where 1 is clinical and cold and 10 is warm and personal — land it at a 7."

That one shift is the difference between using ChatGPT to write emails that sound like you and emails that sound like a template from 2009.

For clinic owners writing patient updates, referral notes, staff communications, or anything that needs to be professional but human, this is how you use ChatGPT for emails without losing your voice. The key is giving it context, not just commands. That's the core of how to write a ChatGPT prompt that actually works: the more specific your brief, the better the output.

It's the same principle behind good creating chatgpt prompts for content creation - and once you see it, you can't unsee it.

videoDetails.shortDescription:
The unknown is the scariest thing.

That's what Jane co-founder Ali Taylor says about fear of change in technology, and it's exactly what holds most clinic owners back from switching software or trying something new. Not the learning curve. Not the cost. The not-knowing.

Ali's analogy: gymnasts standing on a beam. They've done it a thousand times. But the thinking about it - standing at the edge, imagining all the ways it could go wrong - is harder than just stepping up and doing it. That's how to overcome a resistance to change. Not by convincing yourself it's easy. By recognizing that the dread is almost always bigger than the reality.

If you're a clinic owner sitting on the edge of a software switch, or wondering whether ai has a place in your practice, this is the your permission slip!

videoDetails.title: This Is How Clinic Owners Are Using AI for Social Media
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Using AI for social media marketing doesn't mean handing your voice over to a robot. It means having a smarter drafting partner.

In this clip, Destin Jones from the Prehab Guys shares three ways clinic owners are already using chatgpt prompts for social media posts — and none of them require you to post content that sounds like everyone else:
Draft your posts. Use chatgpt for content creation as a starting point, then pull it back into your voice before it goes anywhere near your feed.

Generate topic ideas. Ask what's trending. Use it to break through blank-page paralysis and find angles your audience actually cares about.

Get feedback. Paste in something you've already written and ask AI to sharpen it, simplify it, or push back on it.

If you've been sitting on social media content ideas for your small business and not doing anything with them, this is your under-three-minute case for changing that.

Whether you are a chiropractor, a massage therapist, or any other allied health clinic owner, burnout in private practice often looks the same: too many hats, not enough hours, and a growing feeling that you are keeping it all together with nothing left over.

Private practice management is not about doing more, it’s more about doing less, on purpose, so the right things can get done.

videoDetails.title: The resignation that made this clinic owner become a better leader #janeapp #radiofrontdesk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Two weeks after returning from maternity leave, her clinic manager pulled her into the office and resigned. Dr. Manju describes it as the moment that unraveled everything she thought she knew about her own leadership.

The hard part was not the resignation. It was the self-reflection that followed. She had never done real employee check in questions with the person she relied on most. She knew her clinic manager as a sounding board, as a loyal friend, as someone who said yes to everything. But she had never asked about her sons, her plans, or what she actually needed. That is not a manager employee relationship. That is a one-sided one.

Becoming a better leader in a private practice often starts with a moment like this — not a course, not a framework, but a conversation that forces you to ask: how much do I actually know about the people who carry this clinic?

She also gets honest about boundaries in the workplace and the quiet danger of treating your team like family. The people who care the most will say yes the longest. Until they do not. Knowing how to build relationships with employees that are honest rather than one-sided is what chiropractic practice management and private practice management actually require.

videoDetails.title: How to stop micromanaging as a private practice owner #radiofrontdesk #janeapp
videoDetails.shortDescription:
She took eight months of maternity leave from her private practice, not three months, eight. And the only reason she could was because she had learned how to stop micromanaging and actually trusted her team to run things without her.

In this clip, Dr. Manju walks through the shift in private practice management that made it possible. The signs of micromanaging she recognized in herself were not about being controlling, they were about not knowing how to let go.

Building trust with employees in a clinic is not a team-building exercise. But a decision you make as a practice owner to stop being the bottleneck. When you step back, people step up. That is not a management theory either, that’s what happened here.

Learning to delegate and how to delegate tasks to employees are things most clinic owners know they should do. One meeting a month, a shared scoreboard, and the confidence to know how to manage a clinic that does not rely entirely on you.

videoDetails.title: How to build clinic systems that scale (without facing burnout) #radiofrontdesk #janeapp
videoDetails.shortDescription:
What does it take to build a clinic that runs without you?

Dr. Manju Asdhir is a chiropractor, mentor, and business coach who built and sold not one but three thriving clinics. In this episode of Radio Front Desk, she shares the exact systems, mindset shifts, and private practice management lessons that made it possible, starting with a staff resignation that changed everything.

Her new employee onboarding checklist is one of the most practical frameworks in the episode. It’s a 90-day onboarding plan template that turns nervous new hires into team members who want to stay.

But the onboarding is just one piece, Dr. Manju also walks through how to onboard new employees into a scoreboard system pulled directly from Jane, building a clinic organizational structure that takes the owner out of daily firefighting, creating a culture that earns staff loyalty, and the mindset shift from practitioner to entrepreneur that makes a clinic sellable.

She also shares what buyers actually look for when you are ready to exit, and how to sell a chiropractic practice to someone who can take it further. Plus, the hard lessons on burnout in private practice, setting boundaries with staff, and how to avoid burnout as a business owner by building systems before you need them.

🎙️ Hear the full conversation on Radio Front Desk. The podcast by Jane App for health and wellness practitioners.

Listen here: https://podcasts.apple.com/ca/podcast/radio-front-desk/id1776906866

Jane App is practice management software built for health and wellness practitioners. Learn more at jane.app

#NewEmployeeOnboardingChecklist #OnboardingPlanTemplate #PrivatePracticeManagement #HowToSellAChiropracticPractice #ClinicCulture #BurnoutInPrivatePractice #HowToStopMicromanaging

videoDetails.title: Setting boundaries with employees: a private practice owner's lesson #radiofrontdesk #janeapp
videoDetails.shortDescription:
When a trusted staff member resigned, she turned inward.

In this clip, Dr. Manju reflects on what that moment revealed about her leadership style and the hidden cost of not setting boundaries with employees in a private practice. 

It is one of the most honest conversations about private practice management you will hear from a clinic owner, and it gets at something most health and wellness practitioners feel but rarely say out loud: there is a real difference between running a business and just running the show.

The people who care most about your practice are often the ones most at risk of burning out quietly. For private practice owners, how to keep employees from leaving is less about perks and more about noticing when someone has been giving more than they have been getting.

Staff turnover in healthcare is one of the most common challenges clinic owners face. This clip will not give you a checklist. It will give you something more useful: a moment of honest self-reflection from a practitioner who has been there.

🎙️ Hear the full conversation on Radio Front Desk. The podcast by Jane App for health and wellness practitioners.

Listen here: https://podcasts.apple.com/ca/podcast/radio-front-desk/id1776906866

Jane App is practice management software built for health and wellness practitioners. Learn more at jane.app

videoDetails.title: Staff retention in healthcare starts with day one #radiofrontdesk #janeapp
videoDetails.shortDescription:
In this clip, Dr. Manju walks through the employee career development plan she shows every new hire during the interview, not after they start. 

For new grads, that means a three-month mentorship calendar, 30 minutes every week, scheduled directly into their Jane profile. For seasoned staff, it means 30-day projects in bookkeeping, social, or marketing, and a clear slide deck showing exactly where they can go next.

The whole approach to a career development plan for employees is built around one idea: prove to them that the career path exists before they start wondering if it does. Because in a market this competitive, the ones who cannot see a future will start building their own elsewhere.

This is private practice management that retains people not through loyalty alone but through genuine investment in who they are becoming. And it is one of the most practical frameworks for how to create a career path for employees in a clinic setting.

🎙️ Hear the full conversation on Radio Front Desk. The podcast by Jane App for health and wellness practitioners.

Listen here: https://podcasts.apple.com/ca/podcast/radio-front-desk/id1776906866

videoDetails.title: Do I need a business bank account? An accountant answers #janeapp #radiofrontdesk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
If you own your own small business, this is the first thing an accountant will tell you: get a separate business bank account for sole proprietor use only. Income in, expenses out.

It sounds simple because it is. But commingling your personal and business finances makes bookkeeping for small business more tangled than it needs to be. And come year-end, you’re the one sorting through it alongside your accountant.

Andrew Riesen, co-founder of Heard, walks through the two account setup that sets practitioners up for a smoother tax season. A separate sole proprietor bank account for your business. A separate savings account where your tax money goes every month. If you have heard of the profit first method, this is where it starts. 

Do you need a business bank account? If you are running any kind of practice, even solo, yes. And you can set one up tomorrow!

videoDetails.title: Small business tax deductions you're probably missing #radiofrontdesk #janeapp
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Some write-offs are easy to recognize, others can slip under the radar.

In this clip, accountant and CEO of Heard, Andrew Riesen, breaks down the small business tax deductions most clinic owners and private practitioners overlook, including the startup deduction, home office deductions, and the meals and entertainment deduction that applies every time you share a coffee or a meal for business purposes.

If it looks like a business expense, smells like one, and feels like one, write it down. Tax deductions for therapists and other health and wellness practitioners are more accessible than most people think, and a little awareness now can make a big difference when tax season rolls around.

videoDetails.title: An accountant's guide to bookkeeping for small business #janeapp #radiofrontdesk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
If the words "year-end tax prep" make your eyes glaze over, same. But bookkeeping for a small business doesn't have to be the thing you're always behind on.

In this episode, host Denzil Ford sits down with Andrew Riesen, co-founder of Heard, an accounting platform built for health and wellness practitioners. Andrew shares small business bookkeeping tips that actually stick, and makes the case that getting financially confident is less about becoming an accounting expert and more about building one small habit at a time.

This conversation covers the ground that matters most when it comes to tax planning for your small business, including bookkeeping for healthcare practitioners tax deductions for therapists, and the financial moves clinic owners most commonly put off until it's too late.

Here's what they get into:

- The simple spreadsheet setup for a new practice earning under $15K
- Why how to prepare for tax season starts with one habit, not an overhaul
- Why a business bank account for sole proprietor is step one, and how to set it up tomorrow
- The $75 receipt rule and which categories the IRS looks at most closely
- Home office deduction, continuing education, meals, travel, and marketing, the write-offs practitioners miss most
- When to DIY your small business tax preparation and when to bring in a professional
- What to look for when figuring out how to find a bookkeeper who actually communicates like a human
- The emotional shift that happens when practitioners stop avoiding their numbers, and what life looks like on the other side

🎙️ Hear the full conversation on Radio Front Desk. The podcast by Jane App for health and wellness practitioners.

Listen here: https://podcasts.apple.com/ca/podcast/radio-front-desk/id1776906866

videoDetails.shortDescription:
Most healthcare practitioners didn’t go to school to run a business. And when it comes to small business finances, the fear isn’t about effort. It’s about not knowing what you don’t know.

There is hope if you haven't started yet. The best first step is just trying.

videoDetails.title: Chiropractic marketing tip: the referral secret that fills schedules #janeapp #radiofrontdesk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Josh Satterlee has a chiropractic marketing tip that sounds simple but most practitioners miss it entirely.

When a GP refers a patient to your practice, it's not really the doctor making that happen. It's the front desk staff. That person is the one picking up the phone, sending the fax, writing the email. And if referring to you is easy and they feel genuinely appreciated, they will keep doing it.

Josh knows chiropractors with fewer than ten physician referral/marketing relationships that completely fill their schedule because those relationships never stop sending two, three, four patients a week.

This chiropractic marketing strategy is straightforward: build the relationship with that office, then make referring as frictionless as possible for the person actually doing it. If they fax, you fax. If they email, you email. Whatever works for them. 💙 That's healthcare referral marketing working the way it should, and it's the kind of chiropractic referrals approach that fits into any set of marketing strategies (even if it requires a fax or two).

This clip is from a full conversation on **Radio Front Desk**, the podcast by Jane for health and wellness practitioners.

videoDetails.title: How to Get More Patients Through Referrals | Chiropractic Marketing #radiofrontdesk #janeapp
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Most chiropractic marketing advice tells you to run ads or post on social media. Josh Satterlee chose a referral-first approach.

In this clip, he breaks down his referral-first approach to marketing for chiropractors and why a handful of the right professional relationships can help you fill your schedule. He covers how to identify the right referral partners, how to make it easy for their staff to send patients your way, and the one person in every referral relationship that almost everyone forgets about.

It's one of the most practical marketing ideas out there, and it works whether you're a chiropractor, a physiotherapist, or any other practitioner trying to figure out how to get more patients.

If you've been wondering how to get referrals that actually keep coming, or how to build a referral network that runs itself, this is the place to start.

videoDetails.title: Can ChatGPT be Your Therapist? What AI Means for the Future of Therapy #janeapp #radiofrontdesk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
ChatGPT therapy is already happening. People aren't just using ChatGPT to write emails or plan vacations. They're asking it how to handle their breakup, manage anxiety, or feel less alone at two in the morning when their therapist isn't available.

So can AI replace therapists? The short answer is no, but the longer answer is a lot more nuanced.

Host of Radio Front Desk by Jane App, Denzil Ford, sits down with four clinicians to talk honestly about what's really happening when people turn to AI for emotional support.

They explore why AI mental health support can sometimes feel safer than a human, how therapists are helping clients use it more intentionally, and what they’re hearing from colleagues and other practitioners across the field. It’s a fascinating look at an industry in transition, and what the future of care might hold.

Roxanne Francis – Psychotherapist and Public Speaker
https://www.roxannefrancis.com/

Amanda Buduris – Clinical Psychologist and Business Coach
https://amandakbcoaching.com/

Carolyn Solo – Licensed Clinical Social Worker and Business Coach
https://www.integritaswellnessllc.com/carolyn-solo/

Elana Sures – Clinical Counsellor and Clinic Owner
https://openspacecounselling.ca/

Read the full article "Can ChatGPT Be a Therapist" in Front Desk Vol. 6 at frontdesk.jane.app.

🎙️ Radio Front Desk is the podcast by Jane App for health and wellness practitioners. Listen wherever you get your podcasts: https://podcasts.apple.com/ca/podcast/radio-front-desk/id1776906866

videoDetails.title: How to get more therapy clients through Instagram #radiofrontdesk #janeapp
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Roxanne Francis figured out how to get more therapy clients by just showing up on Instagram. Her approach to therapist marketing was simple: between sessions, she'd pick up her phone and share something on Instagram, answering the questions her followers were already asking about anxiety, relationships, and parenting.

She posted consistently. She posted generously. And slowly, Roxanne became what she calls "the obvious choice."

When someone's cousin needed a therapist, they didn't search directories. They looked up her handle.

That's social media marketing for therapists working the way it should, not like marketing at all, but like showing up for your community. And as a model for private practice marketing, it's about as low-cost and high-return as it gets.

If Roxanne's approach has you thinking differently about how you show up online, there's a full conversation behind this clip.

videoDetails.title: Patient Retention Strategies: How a Better Patient Experience Grows Your Clinic #radiofrontdesk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Graduation day at Emma Jack's clinic looks a little different than most.

Every patient who finishes treatment gets a pin. A small, simple enamel pin. And somehow, that little thing turned into one of her best patient retention strategies. Because people wear them, share them, and talk about the clinic long after their last appointment.

In this clip, she breaks down why the smallest touches in the patient experience are often the best healthcare marketing ideas you'll find. No ad budget needed. Just a moment worth remembering.

If you're looking for low-cost ways to get more patients and build something people actually want to be part of, this one's for you.

videoDetails.title: The Hardest Part of Technology Adoption Isn't the Tech
videoDetails.shortDescription:
The unknown is always scarier than the thing itself.

The fear of change and resistance to change you feel before switching systems is always louder than the transition turns out to be. But practitioners who commit to overcoming fear of new tools are the ones who get their time back.

Whether you're researching practice management software for healthcare for the first time, or you're a solo owner weighing private practice management software, the emotional hurdle is the same. And it is always worth getting through.
If you're also weighing ai adoption challenges in your clinic, the same truth holds. Adopting new technology in business is uncomfortable at first. It always is. The practitioners who push through are the ones who look back and wonder why they waited.

videoDetails.shortDescription:
Amanda Buduris is a trauma psychologist and a self-described introvert. When she was starting her practice, she made a decision that most practitioners don't: she chose SEO over networking.

Amanda taught herself the fundamentals of successful therapy seo, built a website she was proud of, and doubled down on organic search from day one. No referral coffees, no awkward mixers and no spending hours in rooms full of people not knowing if it would ever pay off.

Three years later, every single client who comes to her finds her through Google. She has never spent a cent on a Google ad.

That's what good local seo for therapists actually looks like when it compounds over time. And for anyone doing seo for private practice or wondering whether there's a path to a full caseload without paid ads, Amanda's answer is yes, and she built it herself.

If you're thinking differently about marketing for psychologists after watching this, there's a full conversation behind it.

🎙️ Full conversation on Radio Front Desk, the podcast by Jane App for health and wellness practitioners. Listen here: https://podcasts.apple.com/ca/podcast/radio-front-desk/id1776906866

Jane is practice management software built for allied health. Scheduling, billing, charting, and online booking - all in one place. Learn more at jane.app.

#SEOForTherapists #TherapistSEO #LocalSEOForTherapists #SEOForPrivatePractice #MarketingForPsychologists #PrivatePracticeMarketing #TherapistMarketing #PrivatePracticeGrowth #JaneApp

videoDetails.title: Scaling thoughtfully:  Management insights for chiropractic and physiotherapy clinics #janeapp
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Growing a clinic is exciting, and with the right foundations in place, it can also feel sustainable and rewarding as you look ahead.

​In this Behind the Practice session, host and Jane Ambassador, Genevieve Drummond, sits down with Dr. Hester Potts, clinic owner and the chairperson for the Alliance for Chiropractic, for an honest conversation about what it really looks like to scale a chiro or physio clinic thoughtfully. Whether you’re running a single location or managing multiple practices, this session is about the systems, decisions, and mindset shifts that help clinics grow without sacrificing clinical standards, team culture, or your free time.

​Dr. Hester Potts shares her insights on building and operating thriving practices across both the UK and Canada, including how putting the right foundations in place and using tools helped create clarity, consistency, and breathing room as the business grew.

In this session, we focused on what’s worked, what’s evolved over time, and the lessons Dr. Hester Potts learned along the way to two practices an ocean apart. We’ll discuss how clinic owners can move out of the constant day-to-day firefighting and into a more strategic role in their practice.

​If you’re curious how other clinic owners have approached growth, and want real-world insights you can reflect on and apply in your own way then this is the session for you.
videoDetails.author: Jane

videoDetails.title: Private Practice Marketing That Actually Works #janeapp #radiofrontdesk #marketingforclinics
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Five practitioners, five completely different approaches to private practice marketing, one conversation.

At a recent Jane App retreat, a chiropractor, a psychotherapist, a trauma psychologist, and two physical therapists sat down to talk honestly about how they've grown their practices, without a shared playbook and without burning out in the process.

You'll hear how Emma Jack turned her patient experience into physical therapy marketing that spread entirely through word of mouth. How Josh Satterlee built a chiropractic marketing engine with fewer than 10 key referral relationships — a masterclass in marketing for chiropractors who'd rather go deep than wide. How Amanda Buduris proved that SEO for therapists works: she's been fully booked for three years without spending a dollar on ads. How Roxanne Francis became the obvious choice for her clients by figuring out digital marketing for therapists on Instagram during the pandemic, one story at a time. And how Roni Glassman grew her referral network the old-fashioned way — showing up, in person, again and again.

If you're working out how to get more patients or just looking for healthcare practice marketing ideas that actually feel like you, this one's worth your time. Because the best marketing for therapists, chiropractors, and physical therapists isn't always the loudest — it's the most consistent.

**Chapters:**

- 00:00 Introduction
- 01:30 Emma Jack — Creating a patient experience worth talking about
- 03:00 Josh Satterlee — Building referral relationships that never stop giving
- 05:00 Amanda Buduris — How an introvert built a practice through SEO alone
- 05:45 Roxanne Francis — Becoming the obvious choice on Instagram
- 07:45 Roni Glassman — Door-to-door relationship building that actually works
- 09:00 Closing thoughts

🎙️ Radio Front Desk is the podcast by Jane App for health and wellness practitioners. Listen here: https://podcasts.apple.com/ca/podcast/radio-front-desk/id1776906866

Jane App is practice management software built for health and wellness practitioners. Learn more at jane.app.

videoDetails.title: Improving the Patient Experience Is Your Best Marketing Strategy #janeapp #radiofrontdesk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Turns out the best healthcare marketing strategy might have nothing to do with marketing at all.

Emma Jack is a physio, a business coach, and honestly kind of a genius about this stuff. She built her practice almost entirely through word of mouth. Not by networking harder or running ads, but by focusing on improving patient experience so thoughtfully that people just... couldn't stop talking about it.

In this clip, she walks through the whole framework. The small things that make patients feel like they're part of something. Why her approach to improve patient experience became her most powerful growth tool. And if you've been searching for how to improve patient experience at your own practice, or just trying to figure out how to get more patients without the ads and the pressure, this is the practical alternative.

Worth a watch for anyone in physical therapy marketing or really any corner of health and wellness who wants to grow in a way that actually feels good.

🎙️ Full conversation on Radio Front Desk, the podcast by Jane App for health and wellness practitioners. Listen here: https://podcasts.apple.com/ca/podcast/radio-front-desk/id1776906866

Jane App is practice management software built for health and wellness practitioners. Learn more at https://jane.app/

videoDetails.title: AI in practice: Real ways we're saving time and staying creative #janeapp #janecommunity
videoDetails.shortDescription:
AI is showing up everywhere, but what does it actually look like to use it day-to-day inside your practice?

​In this Ambassador Office Hours session, host Allie Joy is joined by Sean Overin and Emma Furze for an honest, practical conversation about how they’re using AI in their own clinics. From creative planning and business insights, this session is focused on how real practitioners and business owners are using AI to save time.

​Together, they share how AI tools can help guide smarter decisions, or be the starting point for marketing and professional learning, while keeping your work human and aligned with your values.

​If you’re curious about AI but haven’t taken the plunge yet, or you’re wondering how others are using it in their own lives and want grounded examples you can apply, this session is for you.
videoDetails.author: Jane

videoDetails.title: Behind the Practice: Building a purpose-driven practice
videoDetails.shortDescription:
What keeps you showing up for your patients, your team, and your practice when things feel uncertain or overwhelming?

​In this Behind the Practice session, we explore how reconnecting with your purpose can strengthen not only you as a practitioner but also the business you’re building.

​Hosted by Gen Drummond, Jane Ambassador and founder of Hummingbird Clinical Consulting, and featuring Roxanne Francis, psychotherapist and leadership consultant, this conversation looks at what it means to lead a health and wellness practice that’s both sustainable and values-driven.

​Roxanne shares how she rebuilt her approach to leadership and client care after navigating burnout, and how grounding herself in her core values helped her create a thriving, people-centered business.

videoDetails.title: Year-End Metrics: Crunching your data for a thriving practice #janeapp #janecommunity
videoDetails.shortDescription:
​In this Ambassador Office Hours, host Adelle Smener is joined by Robin Valadares, physiotherapist and founder of Financially Fulfilled Physio, and Ildi Arlette, CEO of Results Continuum and leadership coach for health and wellness business owners.

​Together, they dive into the key metrics that matter most for your practice. You’ll learn how to spot hidden profit leaks, evaluate which services are truly sustainable, and make sense of your margins so you can set fees that reflect both your value and your goals.

​The session will also explore how to turn year-end reflections into practical steps for the months ahead. Whether you’re running a solo practice or leading a team, you’ll walk away with simple, actionable ways to use your data to strengthen your business.
videoDetails.author: Jane

What happens when your right-hand team member quits two weeks after you return from maternity leave? For chiropractor and business coach Dr. Manju Asdhir, it was the wake-up call that transformed her clinic — and her leadership.

transcript.text: You see, there's something called the
attribution bias. When we make a
mistake, we tell ourselves, "Oh, I was
in a rush or I didn't sleep or I I was
overwhelmed." When somebody else makes a
mistake, we blame their character. So,
when it's me, it's circumstance. When
it's them, it's character. So, we tend
to blame people as bosses, right? Like,
oh my gosh, really? I really trained
them. But did you? Most of the time,
it's usually a systems problem.

videoDetails.title: Niche down to stand out: Becoming the go-to expert for your clients #janeapp #marketing
videoDetails.shortDescription:
In David’s final session as host of Ambassador Office Hours, he’ll be talking with our guest, Allie Joy, all about how narrowing your focus can help you become the go-to professional in your field. 

They'll share their journeys of finding their niche, along with tips to help you define your unique expertise and attract the clients who need you most.

Whether you’re looking to find your niche, promote your unique services, or just want to connect with fellow practitioners, this session is packed with practical tips and real-world insights.

#FindYourNiche #HealthAndWellnessBusiness #WellnessPracticeGrowth #JaneApp #WellnessEntrepreneur #ClinicOwnerLife #NicheMarketing #WellnessPractitioner #BuildYourPractice #HolisticHealthBusiness #PracticeWithPurpose #GrowYourPractice #WellnessBranding #HealthProTips #WellnessWebinar #CanadianClinics #AlliedHealthProfessionals #JaneCommunity
videoDetails.author: Jane

videoDetails.shortDescription:
What happens when your right-hand team member quits two weeks after you return from maternity leave? For chiropractor and business coach Dr. Manju Asdhir, it was the wake-up call that transformed her clinic — and her leadership.

In this episode, Dr. Manju shares with Denzil how she rebuilt her practice from the inside out. They explore what happens when you stop being the glue holding everything together, and start building systems that let others step up.

videoDetails.title: How a mystery illness sparked a new approach to healthcare: Brandon Jacobs | Park Integrative Health
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Brandon Jacobs often says that without integrative healthcare, his life wouldn’t be the same. A persistent feeling of being “off” led him on a years-long journey to find the source of his symptoms. Brandon was eventually diagnosed with a rare disorder that processes certain foods and turns them into ethanol in the body, making him feel “drunk.”

In this episode, Brandon shares how his personal health journey inspired a unique approach to healthcare by combining primary care with health and wellness — all under one roof.

To check out more stories like Brandon's, head to frontdesk.jane.app.(https://frontdesk.jane.app)

Guest bio

Brandon co-founded Park Integrative Health in 2016, where he currently leads as Director of Allied Health. Brandon’s approach to leadership is based on empathy, and he strives to create a safe, inviting culture that empowers others to thrive. He believes strongly in supporting others on their journey to reach their potential and become the best version of themselves. He seeks to help individuals understand their unique worth as they work towards their goals, whether professional, personal, or health-related.

In 2023, Brandon and the leadership team at Park Integrative Health worked on the build-out and creation of a sister location to house physiotherapy and chiropractic care for the business. Synapse by Park Integrative Health opened its doors in late 2023, and is proud to continue to support the community with a personalized integrative care experience.

Prior to building Park Integrative Health, Brandon worked as a school teacher, a certified personal trainer, a yoga instructor and trainer, and a Registered Massage Therapist.

Guest links

Park Integrative Health on Instagram(https://www.instagram.com/parkintegrative/)
Synapse by Park Integrative Health(https://www.pihealth.ca/synapse)
Brandon Jacobs on Instagram(https://www.instagram.com/brandon.r.jacobs/)
Park Integrative Health on Facebook(https://www.facebook.com/parkintegrativehealth)
Park Integrative Health on LinkedIn(https://www.linkedin.com/company/park-integrative-health/)
Park Integrative Health Wellness Blog(https://www.pihealth.ca/wellnessnotes)
Park Integrative Health Resources + Webinar(https://www.pihealth.ca/resources)

videoDetails.title: Why physical therapists might feel undervalued  #janeapp #frontdesk #podcast
videoDetails.shortDescription:
David Bruton Jr is no stranger to adversity. After eight seasons in the NFL and a long list of football-related injuries, he’s using the skills he learned playing professional sports to forge a new kind of physical therapy practice: one with the good vibes of an NFL locker room.

The physical space is just one way David is doing things differently. In this episode, he tells Denzil about some unexpected choices he’s made, the failures he’s faced, and how his experimentation resulted in something incredible.

videoDetails.title: How a former NFL Career Informs a Thriving Healthcare Business  | David Bruton of Between the Lines
videoDetails.shortDescription:
David Bruton Jr is no stranger to adversity. After eight seasons in the NFL and a long list of football-related injuries, he’s using the skills he learned playing professional sports to forge a new kind of physical therapy practice: one with the good vibes of an NFL locker room.

The physical space is just one way David is doing things differently. In this episode, he tells Denzil about some unexpected choices he’s made, the failures he’s faced, and how his experimentation resulted in something incredible.

To check out more stories like David's, head to frontdesk.jane.app.


As a former NFL veteran, David spent eight years on the gridiron, with seven of those years dedicated to the Denver Broncos, culminating in a thrilling Super Bowl 50 Championship victory. His journey began at the University of Notre Dame, where he honed his skills both on the field and in the classroom, laying the foundation for his future endeavours.

After David’s NFL career, he pursued higher education with the same determination, earning a Doctorate in Physical Therapy from the University of Colorado in 2021. But his commitment to making a difference didn't end there. He founded Bruton's Books in 2015, a literacy foundation aimed at supporting young readers in the Denver Public Schools system, channelling his passion for education into meaningful community impact.

Off the field, David finds joy in cycling, relishing the freedom of the open road. And when he’s not on two wheels, you can often find him lost in the pages of a good book, fuelling his curiosity and expanding his horizons. But above all, his greatest joy comes from spending time with his family, cherishing every moment they share together.

videoDetails.shortDescription:
David Bruton Jr is no stranger to adversity. After eight seasons in the NFL and a long list of football-related injuries, he’s using the skills he learned playing professional sports to forge a new kind of physical therapy practice: one with the good vibes of an NFL locker room.

The physical space is just one way David is doing things differently. In this episode, he tells Denzil about some unexpected choices he’s made, the failures he’s faced, and how his experimentation resulted in something incredible.

videoDetails.shortDescription:
In the world of business, many people say faster is better. More clients means more profit, and growth is good. While this advice works for some, it’s a notion Eric Vermander and his wife Caroline King are constantly pushing against.

In this episode, we talk about what practicing patience looks like in business and the hard work that goes into bringing your vision to life.

videoDetails.title: Does better always mean faster? | Owners of Vitae Health & Sport talk sustainable business growth
videoDetails.shortDescription:
In the world of business, many people say faster is better. More clients means more profit, and growth is good. While this advice works for some, it’s a notion Eric Vermander and his wife Caroline King are constantly pushing against.

Owners of Vitae Health & Sport in North Vancouver, Eric and Caroline are a husband-wife duo running their clinic for the long haul. They’ve taken a slower, yet intentional approach with their clinic, allowing them to craft a collaborative staff culture and a clinic experience that clients are organically attracted to.

In this episode, we talk about what practicing patience looks like in business and the hard work that goes into bringing your vision to life.

To check out more stories like Eric and Caroline's, head to https://frontdesk.jane.app/.



Vitae Health & Sport is the result of a blend of ideas, experiences, and a shared vision. Caroline King grew up in the friendly Midwest, earning her degree in mass communication from Miami University in Ohio. She sharpened her organizational skills in New York City's advertising scene before pursuing a Master's of Education from Loyola University Baltimore. Ready for a new challenge, she embraced the entrepreneurial spirit.

Dr. Eric Vermander grew up in Ottawa, Ontario, and turned his passion for sports and cycling into a career while studying Human Kinetics at the University of Ottawa. All along, he spent over 14 years in the sports retail industry. His journey led him from the Canadian Rockies to the University of Western States in Portland, Oregon, where he pursued chiropractic medicine. He continued to gain valuable clinical experience in Vancouver, British Columbia for a decade.

Together, as a couple with a daughter in tow, they opened Vitae Health & Sport in North Vancouver in 2019. Since then, they’ve focused on cultivating relationships, growing their business, and becoming a trusted part of the community—all while learning and evolving along the way.

Vitae Health & Sport
Vitae Health & Sport on Instagram
Vitae Health & Sport on Facebook
In this episode

Vancity Credit Union


This podcast is for informational purposes only and should not be considered as professional medical, legal, or financial advice. The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Jane or Front Desk magazine.

videoDetails.shortDescription:
David Bruton Jr is no stranger to adversity. After eight seasons in the NFL and a long list of football-related injuries, he’s using the skills he learned playing professional sports to forge a new kind of physical therapy practice: one with the good vibes of an NFL locker room.

The physical space is just one way David is doing things differently. In this episode, he tells Denzil about some unexpected choices he’s made, the failures he’s faced, and how his experimentation resulted in something incredible.

videoDetails.shortDescription:
Matt Lundquist didn’t always want to be a psychotherapist. Initially, he saw himself as more of a philosopher and social justice advocate — someone working to make change in his community. 

But, in doing that, he found a passion for clinical work, appreciating the one-on-one connections he made with people and the direct impact he could have on their lives. That’s when he realized: “Wait a minute, I think I’m a therapist.” Today, Matt is the founder and clinical director of Tribeca Therapy in New York City. 

In this episode, Denzil learns how Matt’s early leadership trials helped build a thriving, people-first therapy practice.
videoDetails.author: Jane

videoDetails.title: Knowing your front desk before hiring #businessowner #frontdesk #radiofrontdesk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Joseph Ibe is a seasoned chiropractor, business owner, and investor - what some call a multi-hyphenate, and for good reason. He’s spent time building and flipping clinics, breathing new life into businesses that need it, and giving the next generation of practitioners a stake in the ground.

In this episode of Radio Front Desk, Denzil learns about what it *actually* takes to get started building a practice. Spoiler alert - according to Joseph, it’s not as scary as you might think.
Subscribe and Listen to Radio Front Desk here:

https://radiofrontdesk.buzzsprout.com/

videoDetails.title: Achieving success in the new year with effective goal-setting
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Join David Bruton in this extra-special handover session with Ambassador Office Hours' previous host, Kendall Hagensen from Wellness Center Creators, to talk about how effective goal setting early on can help you achieve both big and small goals for you and your practice.

In this session they discuss how they approach their goals, what things they consider when setting those goals, and how staff, finances, and other factors play a vital role in achieving new year practice goals.

#janeapp #practicemanagement #businessowner #goalsettingtips #goals #goalsetting #goalsettingmotivation #goalsettingforsuccess #goalsetting2025 #worklifebalance #worklifeintegration #worklifebalancegoals  #emrs #chiropractor #mentalhealth #mentalhealthprofessionals #bussinessmindset #businesstips #practitioner #businessgrowth #businessoptimization #scheduling #worklifebalance #worklife #worklifeintegration

videoDetails.title: What does it really take to start a practice? Joseph Ibe: Chiropractor, business owner, and investor
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Joseph Ibe⁠ is a seasoned chiropractor, business owner, and investor - what some call a multi-hyphenate, and for good reason. He’s spent time building and flipping clinics, breathing new life into businesses that need it, and giving the next generation of practitioners a stake in the ground.
In this episode of Radio Front Desk, Denzil learns about what it actually takes to get started building a practice. Spoiler alert - according to Joseph, it’s not as scary as you might think.

To check out more stories like Joseph's, head to ⁠frontdesk.jane.app⁠.(frontdesk.jane.app)


⁠Dr. Joseph Ibe⁠ is a Blair Upper Cervical Chiropractor with 14 years of experience in private practice and a serial entrepreneur focused on health-related ventures. He thrives at the intersection of technology and patient care, always seeking innovative ways to improve outcomes. Driven by an unwavering commitment to the patient experience, Dr. Ibe believes exceptional care is the foundation of every healthcare provider’s mission. His vision of a healthier future is built on utilizing technology to advance well-being, stating, “I believe in a healthier world with healthier people utilizing technology to advance their wellbeing.”

In addition to growing his practice, Dr. Ibe has ventured into the startup scene, enterprise-level projects, and academia, gaining valuable insights from each sector. His work reflects a belief that the pursuit of serving others is endless and that healthcare professionals should continually evolve, learn, and apply their skills across various industries.
Dr. Ibe is passionate about creating solutions that blend technology and humanity, helping to shape the future of healthcare. His enduring mission is to inspire others to strive for excellence and to pursue service to their communities with relentless dedication.


⁠Joseph Ibe on Instagram⁠ (https://www.instagram.com/josephibe/)


This podcast is for informational purposes only and should not be considered as professional medical, legal, or financial advice. The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Jane or Front Desk magazine. Listener discretion is advised. This episode contains mature content and language that may not be suitable for all audiences.

videoDetails.title: "Wait a minute, I think I’m a therapist”: Matt Lundquist’s unique path to practice | Tribeca Therapy
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Matt Lundquist didn’t always want to be a psychotherapist. Initially, he saw himself as more of a philosopher and social justice advocate — someone working to make change in his community.



But, in doing that, he found a passion for clinical work, appreciating the one-on-one connections he made with people and the direct impact he could have on their lives. That’s when he realized: “Wait a minute, I think I’m a therapist.” Today, Matt is the founder and clinical director of Tribeca Therapy in New York City.



In this episode, Denzil learns how Matt’s early leadership trials helped build a thriving, people-first therapy practice.



Read more stories like Matt’s over at frontdesk.jane.app.



Matt is the Founder and Clinical Director of Tribeca Therapy, a 15-year-old group psychotherapy practice in Lower Manhattan, New York City. Matt is the lead supervisor for the practice and views his primary task as supporting talented therapists to become thriving clinicians and supervisors.



Matt trained in both psychodynamic and postmodern therapies and has extensive experience working with systems including couples, families and groups. Matt has provided organizational consulting to Fortune-500 companies and nonprofits and has served as an adjunct clinical instructor at the Hunter College School of Social Work. In addition to managing and providing clinical leadership for the practice, Matt maintains an active practice with individuals and couples.



Tribeca Therapy Team(https://tribecatherapy.com/our-therapists/about/)
Tribeca Therapy Website Tribeca(https://tribecatherapist.com/)
Therapy on Instagram(https://www.instagram.com/tribecatherapy/)
Tribeca Therapy on X (formerly Twitter)(https://x.com/tribecatherapy)
Tribeca Therapy on TikTok(https://www.tiktok.com/@tribecatherapy)


This podcast is for informational purposes only and should not be considered as professional medical, legal, or financial advice. The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Jane or Front Desk magazine.

videoDetails.shortDescription:
Joseph Ibe is a seasoned chiropractor, business owner, and investor - what some call a multi-hyphenate, and for good reason. He’s spent time building and flipping clinics, breathing new life into businesses that need it, and giving the next generation of practitioners a stake in the ground.

In this episode of Radio Front Desk, Denzil learns about what it *actually* takes to get started building a practice. Spoiler alert - according to Joseph, it’s not as scary as you might think.

videoDetails.title: You can have everything, but not all at once #management #businessowner #radiofrontdesk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Dr. Julie Durnan is a dual entrepreneur doing something pretty unique: she’s a licensed naturopathic physician who’s opened not one, but two businesses. 

The first was a naturopathic clinic in North Vancouver. Then, when practitioners retreated online during the pandemic, she launched a second business - Ginger Desk - a virtual assistant company now serving hundreds of clinics. 

In this episode, Denzil talks to Julie about balancing the demands of business ownership, improving the patient journey through admin, advocating for women and her staff, and her creative strategies for efficient and successful clinics.

videoDetails.title: Rethinking the front desk: Why personalized service isn’t always better Dr. Julie Durnan Ginger Desk
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Dr. Julie Durnan is a dual entrepreneur doing something pretty unique: she’s a licensed naturopathic physician who’s opened not one, but two businesses.

The first was a naturopathic clinic in North Vancouver. Then, when practitioners retreated online during the pandemic, she launched a second business - Ginger Desk - a virtual assistant company now serving hundreds of clinics.

In this episode, Denzil talks to Julie about balancing the demands of business ownership, improving the patient journey through admin, advocating for women and her staff, and her creative strategies for efficient and successful clinics.

Read how Julie got started building GingerDesk in Front Desk Magzine

Dr. Julie Durnan is the Founder and CEO of Ginger Desk - virtual administrative assistants for health practitioners. Ginger Desk hires, trains, and manages industry-trained medical office assistants and matches these Virtual Assistants (VAs) to health practitioners remotely. Their ultimate goal is to empower solo practitioners and clinic owners to achieve administrative freedom.

Dr. Julie Durnan has founded two integrative health clinics and currently operates one of them with a team of 10 practitioners. Over nearly 20 years, she has been deeply involved in running clinics and maintaining a private naturopathic medical practice. She is a community builder and a fierce advocate for supporting women who want to build their careers, focus on their families, and make health services more accessible for all.



GingerDesk
GingerDesk on Instagram
GingerDesk on Facebook
GingerDesk on LinkedIn


This podcast is for informational purposes only and should not be considered as professional medical, legal, or financial advice.

The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of the podcast host or its affiliates.

videoDetails.title: How to beat practice and business owner burnout and stress
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Join your host Kendall Hagensen as she sits down with fellow Jane Community member and Ambassador Genevieve Drummond, owner of Hummingbird Clinical Consulting LLC, to chat about how clinic stress starts and how they combat it themselves and with their teams.

In this session, they discuss the familiar feeling of being overwhelmed by heavy practice workloads and the resulting stress. They reflect on inefficient work habits that contribute to their daily challenges and share the workflows they've implemented to overcome them.

#janeapp #practicemanagement #businessowner #burnout #stress #stressmanagement #worklifebalance #worklifeintegration #worklifebalancegoals  #emrs #chiropractor #mentalhealth #mentalhealthprofessionals #bussinessmindset #businesstips #practitioner #businessgrowth #businessoptimization #scheduling #worklifebalance #worklife #worklifeintegration
videoDetails.author: Jane

videoDetails.title: Salary vs. commission: Creating a better experience for your team #radiofrontdesk #businessowner
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Walk into Aphrodite Fertility Acupuncture, and you’ll be greeted by vibrant plants, a warm waiting room, and a soft piano track playing over the speakers. Regardless of your journey to Aphrodite, you’re welcomed into a safe space, carefully built for some of life’s most vulnerable moments.

In this episode, you’ll hear how Emily and Ghoncheh’s trust for each other as business partners overflows to their practice, and how they make the guest experience a healing one.

Subscribe and Listen to Radio Front Desk here:

https://radiofrontdesk.buzzsprout.com/

videoDetails.title: Does your clinic have a mission? | Emily Marson & Ghoncheh Ayazi of Aphrodite Fertility
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Walk into ⁠Aphrodite Fertility Acupuncture(http://www.aphroditefertility.com/)⁠, and you’ll be greeted by vibrant plants, a warm waiting room, and a soft piano track playing over the speakers. Regardless of your journey to this fertility clinic, you’re welcomed into a safe space, carefully built for some of life’s most vulnerable moments.

In this episode, you’ll hear how Emily Marson and Ghoncheh Ayazi’s trust for each other as business partners overflows to their practice, and how they make their guest experience a healing one.

Learn more about Aphrodite Fertility, and read their story ⁠here⁠(https://frontdesk.jane.app/articles/marketing-done-right-aphrodite-fertility) in Front Desk magazine.

Emily Marson is a true believer in comprehensive integrative fertility care. She has made it her mission to pull women out of the darkness of infertility and guide them to the family they’ve always wanted. She credits much of her patient’s success to combining the ancient wisdom of Chinese medicine with modern, conventional biomedicine. You will find just what you need at Aphrodite, every step of the way.

Ghoncheh Ayazi is deeply committed to empowering and guiding women and couples on their journey towards a happy, healthy baby. Her life’s mission is to support women through these major life transitions equipped with knowledge and support. She is a leading specialist in integrative fertility, hormone and menstrual health, pregnancy and postpartum wellness. Ghoncheh loves connecting deeply with her patients one-on-one to lend a listening ear, provide thoughtful questions and guide them to the rightful next steps to help reach a healthy pregnancy. She is a cherished resource for those seeking support and care on their fertility journey.

⁠Aphrodite Fertility⁠(https://aphroditefertility.com/)
⁠Aphrodite on Instagram⁠(https://www.instagram.com/aphroditefertility/)
⁠Aphrodite Movement Studio⁠(https://aphroditemovement.com/)
⁠Aphrodite Movement on Instagram⁠(https://www.instagram.com/aphrodite.movement/)

This podcast is for informational purposes only and does not constitute medical, legal, or financial advice. Please consult with a professional for specific advice tailored to your situation.

The views expressed in this podcast are solely those of the host/guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of any agency or company.Listener discretion is advised. 

This episode contains material that may be triggering or distressing for some individuals. We recommend that listeners exercise caution and consider skipping this episode if they anticipate that the content may adversely affect their well-being.

Listener discretion is advised. This episode contains explicit (or mature content and/or language that may not be suitable for all audiences.

videoDetails.title: Bootstrapping a PT business in a fiercely competitive market | Dr. Kellen Scantlebury of Fit Club NY
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Bootstrapping a business in New York City — famously known as one of the toughest and most competitive places in the world to succeed — is no small feat.

In this episode, we hear from Dr. Kellen Scantlebury — a physical therapist who’s built his practice, Fit Club New York, from the ground up. Kellen shares how he developed his unique brand philosophy, the things he does to set himself apart in a saturated market, and what he’s learned in scaling his practice.

To check out more stories like Kellen's, head to https://jane.app/frontdesk

Dr. Kellen Scantlebury received his Doctor of Physical Therapy degree from Stony Brook University. He received his Bachelor's of Science degree from Long Island University Post, where he also was a four-year starter on the men's basketball team. Upon graduating from LIU, Kellen's passion for fitness and desire to help others inspired him to pursue a personal training certification from the American College of Sports Medicine. It was in these years that the foundation for Fit Club was established.

Kellen started Fit Club Physical Therapy in 2017 with the mission of elevating the physical therapy experience for the active individual in New York. Since embarking on this journey he has helped thousands of people and opened four clinics in the New York area. Kellen has given presentations at Fortune 500 companies with regards to the latest evidence-based practice on prevention of injury for the runner and ergonomic set up. In his free time, Kellen stays active playing intramural basketball, weightlifting, and running. He also enjoys spending time with his family, friends and traveling.

Fit Club NY https://fitclubny.com/

Dr. Kels on Instagram https://www.instagram.com/dr.kels

Fit Club NY on Instagram https://www.instagram.com/fitclubny



This podcast is for informational purposes only and should not be considered as professional medical, legal, or financial advice.

The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of the podcast host or its affiliates.

videoDetails.title: Ending the professional relationship and navigating challenging patients #janeapp #practicemgmt
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Join your fellow community member and Jane Ambassador Genevieve Drummond, founder of Hummingbird Clinical Consulting LLC, for a chat with special guest Roxanne Francis, CEO of Francis Psychotherapy, about how they navigate tricky patient situations.

In this session they chat about how they set up practice policies for dealing with patients who regularly overbook, cancel at the last minute, or just don't show up.They'll also share the systems they've put in place to empower both themselves and their teams, helping everyone they work with feel confident when it comes to setting boundaries or, at times, even ending a professional relationship.

#janeapp #practicemanagement #businessowner #marketingdigital  #marketing #marketingtips #marketingstrategy #seo #seotips  #emrs #chiropractor #mentalhealth #mentalhealthprofessionals #aesthetic #aestheticpracticemarketinghelp  #bussinessmindset #businesstips #practitioner #businessgrowth #businessoptimization #scheduling #worklifebalance #worklife #worklifeintegration
